name: Sweden
contextBlurb: Sweden wants a top-ten spot in global AI — adopting its first national AI Strategy in February 2026 and implementing the EU AI Act via complementary legislation targeted for August 2026. Cold-climate free-air cooling and Norrbotten hydropower have made it one of Europe's premier green data center destinations, anchored by Meta's Luleå hyperscale campus. A concurrent push to authorize AI facial recognition for police (Proposition 2025/26:150) is drawing civil-liberties scrutiny.

legislation.title: Complementary Swedish Legislation to Implement the EU AI Act
legislation.summary: Published in October 2025, this Government Official Report proposes a new national Swedish AI law and ordinance designating the Post and Telecom Authority (PTS) as the primary market surveillance authority, with 11 sectoral co-authorities, enforceable administrative fines, and at least one regulatory sandbox to be established by August 2, 2026. It bridges EU AI Act mandates with Swedish administrative procedures on secrecy, documentation, and enforcement powers.

legislation.title: Act on the Disclosure of Information on the Energy Performance of Data Centres
legislation.summary: Enacted and in force from July 1, 2025, this law requires owners or operators of data centers with an installed IT power demand of at least 500 kW to publicly report annual energy performance data to the EU-wide database, implementing the EU Energy Efficiency Directive (EED). Facilities used for security-sensitive or defense purposes under the Protective Security Act are exempt.

legislation.title: Proposition on Police Use of AI-Powered Real-Time Facial Recognition
legislation.summary: Submitted to the Riksdag in March 2026, this proposition proposes authorizing Swedish police to deploy AI-driven real-time biometric identification in public spaces for serious crimes such as human trafficking and murder, making Sweden one of the first EU member states to explicitly legislate live biometric surveillance by law enforcement. The bill, referred to the Committee on Justice, is designed to operationalize the narrow EU AI Act exceptions for law enforcement while including legal safeguards for personal privacy.

keyFigures.name: Erik Slottner
keyFigures.role: Minister for Public Administration, Ministry of Finance
keyFigures.quote: Sweden aims to be a leader in AI, which is why it's important that we take a comprehensive approach to AI issues.

keyFigures.name: Gunnar Strömmer
keyFigures.quote: To push back gang crime and restore security in society, the police must have access to effective tools.
